markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Define document IDs, titles, project/folder membership, authority labels, owner actors, and schema versions. This MT belongs to RichDocumentCore. Build the canonical structured document model backing the editor, Loom, UserManual, and projections.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Rich documents are PostgreSQL/EventLedger/CRDT-backed structured block documents, not Markdown vault authority.
All rich document state must preserve Raw/Derived/Display separation and stable entity IDs.
scope.expected_behavior: RichDocumentIdentityModel leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: RichDocumentIdentityModel is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: Built+verified (commits b506d832 backend core + 935e0482 manual + bf0c62a5 integration tests + dd3056f5 frontend); RichDocumentCore: 6 real-PG integration + 27 unit backend tests + 8 vitest (app suite 349/349). 18 block kinds, RawDerivedDisplay (CX-100), stable KBL- ids, save/load API w/ receipts, projections (negative proof: projection-delete leaves authority byte-identical), embeds (absolute paths DB-CHECK rejected), backlinks (stable KDLNK- relationship_id), history, permissions, crash recovery; migrations 0280-0282; RichDocumentView.tsx wired to real authority with stable selectors for MT-161+ GUI groups. UserManual coverage added (1 group + 10 cmd refs).

adversarial_review_v2.note: Identity validation solid: DB CHECK closed vocab for authority_label + owner_actor_kind, owner-pair all-or-nothing, project_ref/folder_ref reject absolute/drive-letter paths (0280.sql:41-84). owner_actor_kind is self-asserted via header but that exposure is owned by MT-158.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-145 (RichDocumentIdentityModel) requires document IDs, titles, project/folder membership, authority labels, owner actors, and schema versions backed by PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority. The code delivers this via migration 0280 (additive identity columns with fail-closed CHECK constraints), real PostgreSQL storage ops (create/rename/move/set_authority_label/list), API wiring that stamps owner identity from actor context and leaves EventLedger receipts, and real Handshake-managed-PostgreSQL integration tests including a fail-closed negative case.
findings: Migration src/backend/handshake_core/migrations/0280_knowledge_document_identity.sql:34-90 EXTENDS the MT-059 knowledge_rich_documents table with project_ref, folder_ref, authority_label (NOT NULL DEFAULT 'promoted'), owner_actor_kind, owner_actor_id; all additive/safe. Closed-vocab CHECKs on authority_label (draft|promoted|archived, line 45-46) and owner_actor_kind (line 52-55), all-or-nothing owner pair CHECK (line 61-63), and path-rejecting CHECKs on project_ref/folder_ref (no leading '/', no drive-letter, trimmed non-empty, line 70-84). Membership indexes added (line 87-90). Fail-closed identity, matches spec 2.3.13.11 PG/EventLedger authority requirement.

Storage authority is real PostgreSQL: src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/knowledge.rs:4882 create_knowledge_rich_document binds the 5 identity columns, validates authority_label vocab (line 4907) and owner all-or-nothing (line 4912) in Rust before insert, generates KRD- ids, and lands revision 1 in append-only history. rename (5322), move (5349), set_authority_label (5373), list with NULL-safe optional project/folder scoping (5400) are all genuine sqlx UPDATE/SELECT against the real table, returning typed rows.

API layer src/backend/handshake_core/src/api/knowledge_documents.rs wires identity end-to-end: create_document (line 659) routes owner_actor_kind/owner_actor_id from the actor context (ctx.actor, line 671-672), move_document (1464-1500) and the batch SetAuthorityLabel/move ops (1553-1580) call the real storage methods, and saves leave KNOWLEDGE_RICH_DOCUMENT_SAVED EventLedger receipts (header comment line 8, append helper line 424). Module header explicitly states 'EventLedger authority (NO mocks, no SQLite)'.

Runtime proof is real Handshake-managed PostgreSQL, not a mock: tests/knowledge_pg_support.rs resolves POSTGRES_TEST_URL/DATABASE_URL or falls back to managed_postgres::ManagedPostgres::ensure_running (default port 5544, Handshake_Artifacts/managed_pgdata), creates an isolated schema, runs migrations, and connects a real PostgresDatabase (line 153). tests/knowledge_document_model_tests.rs exercises MT-145 identity: end_to_end (line 82-93) asserts project_ref/folder_ref/authority_label/owner round-trip; identity_validation_fails_closed (line 688-715) asserts a bad authority_label ('published') and a half-owner both return typed StorageError::Validation; import_markdown_then_batch_rename_and_move (596-661) proves rename does not bump version and move updates project/folder with membership listing.

No placeholders or deferred-live escapes: grep for todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/'not yet wired'/'deferred to follow-on'/'pending MT-'/Unavailable across src/backend/handshake_core/src/knowledge_document/ and migration 0280 returned zero hits. Aside (non-blocking, paperwork): packet cites v02.192 spec anchors while current is v02.194; the same section 2.3.13.11 / 7.1.1.8 content exists in v02.194 so alignment holds. The adversarial note that owner_actor_kind is self-asserted via header is explicitly owned by MT-158, out of MT-145 scope.
evidence_checked: git show --stat b506d832 (backend core) + bf0c62a5 (integration tests)

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
